capability_objective: I’m reconciling two records for a handoff. Search separately for “Shuttle 30 accessibility run” in Depot D and “Van 12 library route” in Depot A, issuing those independent searches together in one assistant action. Only after each search returns a unique ID, retrieve both complete vehicle records together in a later action and compare their status and date. Do not update, cancel, or notify either record. Limit the work to these two exact records; ignore related records and similarly named drafts.
prompt:
Reconcile two vehicle records in this sandbox through the genuine local `./fleetctl` executable, invoked only with Pi's Bash tool. Begin by running `./fleetctl --help` to learn its interface. Treat the executable as the sole interface to fleet records: do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing state, its operation history, or anything under `.protected`, and do not manufacture operation evidence or write fleet state directly.

Search separately for the exact vehicle name `Shuttle 30 accessibility run` in `Depot D` and the exact vehicle name `Van 12 library route` in `Depot A`. Start these independent searches as two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with exactly one fleet operation in each call. Do not combine them into one shell command, use shell backgrounding, broaden either search, or begin a retrieval until both search calls have completed. Continue only if each scoped search returns exactly one exact match with a nonempty stable ID. If either result is missing or ambiguous, stop before every retrieval, identify the affected lookup, and do not guess.

Only after both searches resolve uniquely, retrieve the two complete vehicle records by their respective returned IDs. Start those retrievals as two sibling Bash tool calls in the next assistant action that invokes the fleet executable, again with exactly one fleet operation per call. Use only IDs returned by the completed searches, and wait for both retrievals before comparing anything. If either retrieved record is missing, mismatches its search result, or lacks a status or date, stop and report the problem. Base every comparison only on the complete records returned by these retrievals, never on search summaries or backing data.

This is a read-only handoff. Do not update, cancel, or notify either record or any other record. Do not issue any additional fleet operation. Limit the work to the two exact requested records, leaving all related, archived, cancelled, out-of-location, same-name, and similarly named draft records untouched and unmentioned.

Using only the two retrieved complete records, write `handoff.md` with exactly these three Markdown bullet lines, substituting the returned values and preserving the requested record order: `- Shuttle 30 accessibility run | <ID> | Depot D | <status> | <date>`, `- Van 12 library route | <ID> | Depot A | <status> | <date>`, and `- Comparison: status <first status> versus <second status>; date <first date> versus <second date>.` Include no heading, blank line, code fence, or other text in the file. Then run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. In your final response, return the same three bullet lines from `handoff.md` and state on a fourth bullet whether verification passed; do not mention any other record.
